prevalence.source: Primary care and pediatric infection epidemiology syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable; seasonal peaks in school periods

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical assessment (Centor/McIsaac in adolescents/adults), rapid antigen test/culture when available to confirm streptococcal infection; evaluate for complications (peritonsillar abscess) and alarm signs

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; symptom-driven evaluation in APS/UPA
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High for symptom relief; antibiotic stewardship depends on diagnostic access

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis common; confirmatory testing variably available in public settings
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care for viral; antibiotics for confirmed/suspected streptococcal disease (penicillin/amoxicillin); tonsillectomy evaluation for recurrent severe cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Overuse of antibiotics without testing in some settings; access to ENT for recurrent cases
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Effective first-line antibiotics widely available; robust primary care access in many municipalities
